channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Germans mourn the 5 killed and 200 injured in the apparent attack on a Christmas market
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Germans on Saturday mourned the victims of an apparent attack in which authorities say a doctor drove into a busy outdoor Christmas market, killing five people, injuring 200 others and shaking the public’s sense of security at what would otherwise be a time of joy.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, GEIR MOULSON and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A rocket from Yemen strikes Tel Aviv, injuring 16, and Palestinians mourn a dozen children in Gaza
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — A rocket fired from Yemen hit an area of Tel Aviv overnight, leaving 16 people injured by shattered glass, the Israeli military said Saturday, days after Israeli airstrikes hit Houthi rebels who have been launching missiles in solidarity with Palestinians in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and WAAFA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What we know about the deadly Christmas market attack in Germany
item.description: Officials say five people have been killed, including a 9-year-old, after a Saudi doctor drove into a Christmas market teeming with holiday shoppers in the German city of Magdeburg on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: German Christmas market ramming is the latest attack to use vehicles as deadly weapons
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A car-ramming at a Christmas market in Germany, which police are treating as an attack, is the latest in a grim series of events in which vehicles have been used as deadly weapons.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Hanukkah and how it’s celebrated around the world
item.description: Hanukkah — also spelled Chanukah or other transliterations from Hebrew — is Judaism’s “festival of lights.”.
item.dc:creator: Giovanna Dell'Orto, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: France's Mayotte struggles to recover as cyclone overwhelms hospitals
item.description: A week after Cyclone Chido ravaged Mayotte, France’s poorest territory, the island remains in crisis.
item.dc:creator: Sam Mednick, Adrienne Surprenant And Thomas Adamson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Indonesians mark 2 decades since the tragic tsunami that killed hundreds of thousands
item.description: Two decades after a catastrophic tsunami destroyed her village, Tria Asnani still cries when she recalls how she lost her mother while trying to escape the giant waves and her father to the sea.
item.dc:creator: Edna Tarigan, Achmad Ibrahim, And Fadlan Syam, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Malaysia agrees to resume 'no find, no fee' hunt for flight MH370, 10 years after plane disappeared
item.description: Over 10 years after flight MH370 is believed to have crashed in the Indian Ocean, Malaysia's government has agreed to accept a "no find, no fee" proposal from a U.S. company to resume the hunt.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden missing in action as Turkey inches closer to full-blown war against US-allied Kurds in Syria
item.description: Amid the end of the nearly 14 years of civil war in Syria, Turkish strongman Recep Tayyip Erdoğan is set to rekindle a new war in northern Syria against America’s main ally, the Syrian Kurds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amazon Indigenous community wins latest stage of court battle for lost territory
item.description: An Indigenous community in the Peruvian Amazon is celebrating a legal victory in the latest stage of its attempt to take back claimed ancestral rainforests. The Puerto Franco community of the Kichwa tribe say their territory was stolen to form the Cordillera Azul National Park in 2001.
item.dc:creator: ED DAVEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 dead, many injured after man drives into German Christmas market in suspected terror attack: report
item.description: A festive Christmas market in Madgeburg, Germany erupted into chaos after a car was driven into a group of people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Support for quick end to Russia-Ukraine war grows in US: Gallup
item.description: Americans' desire to speed up the end of the Russia-Ukraine war has ticked up, a new Gallup survey found.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Italy's privacy watchdog fines OpenAI for ChatGPT's violations in collecting users personal data
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s data protection watchdog said Friday it has fined OpenAI 15 million euros ($15.6 million) after wrapping up a probe into the collection of personal data by the U.S. artificial intelligence company's popular chatbot ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Malaysia resuming search for MH370 wreckage
item.description: Malaysian officials approved a third probe into the Indian Ocean with the goal of recovering wreckage from flight MH370, which disappeared more than a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Chinese national charged with acting as Beijing's agent in local California election
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Chinese national was arrested Thursday on charges of acting as an illegal agent for Beijing when serving as the campaign manager for an unnamed politician elected to a city council in Southern California two years ago.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, AMY TAXIN and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens tariffs if EU doesn't buy more oil, gas
item.description: President-elect Trump on Friday threatened tariffs in the European Union (EU) unless the bloc purchases large quantities of American oil and gas to make up for its growing deficit with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US diplomats in Damascus for first time in more than 10 years following fall of Assad regime
item.description: U.S. officials are in Syria with goals to advance Aqaba principles and seek information on American citizens who disappeared under the Assad regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blinken says 'pressure is on Hamas' to move forward with ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken said the pressure is on the Palestinian militant group Hamas to move forward with the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip, arguing the group’s support from its allies in the region has dwindled in recent months.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: 5 takeaways from Putin's annual press conference
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin talked for more than three hours during an annual news conference in Moscow on Thursday, discussing key topics including the war in Ukraine, his interest in dialogue with President-elect Trump and an openness to asking about detained journalist Austin Tice in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Palestinian Authority under pressure amid rising resistance, popularity of Iran-backed terror groups
item.description: Palestinian Authority’s crackdown on terrorists intensifies, but efforts to regain control of the city and surrounding areas face fierce resistance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Frenchman found guilty in horrific rape trial that shocked the world
item.description: A Frenchman accused of drugging his wife of 50 years and inviting strangers over to rape her – while he filmed the assaults – was found guilty on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China skirts US efforts to stiff-arm CCP interference by bolstering state, local relations
item.description: China is skirting U.S. efforts to block its interference in areas ranging from trade to national security by cozying up with state officials to foster relations and keep its influence active in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pro-Palestinian activists storm basketball court during Israeli team’s game against France
item.description: Anti-Israel agitators disrupted an Israeli-French basketball game Wednesday, waving Palestinian flags and sparking chaos in the stands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia's Syria exit could help Ukraine-Israel relationship as analyst warns it 'offers little' to Jerusalem
item.description: With Russia’s diminished influence in Syria, Ukraine calls on Israel to reconsider its stance and provide essential defensive weapons and technology.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia detains suspect accused of killing high-ranking general in Moscow
item.description: A suspect has been taken into custody in connection with the Tuesday killing of Lt. Gen. Igor Kirillov and his assistant in Moscow, according to Russian officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu says Israeli troops will occupy a buffer zone inside Syria for the foreseeable future
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Tuesday that Israeli forces will stay in a buffer zone on the Syrian border, seized after the ouster of Syria's President Bashar Assad, until another arrangement is in place “that ensures Israel's security.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Blast kills 2 Mexican soldiers and wounds 5 others in suspected booby trap by drug cartel
item.description: While approaching, they spotted three dismembered bodies near the encampment, which appeared to be abandoned. But as they drew closer, a buried device exploded and struck the soldiers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: FIFA apologizes to Ukraine soccer body for Crimea map error at 2026 World Cup draw
item.description: “While the incident was unintentional, we sincerely regret any concern it may have cause,” FIFA’s chief member association officer wrote in a letter .
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US officials have been in direct contact with the Syrian rebel group that ousted Assad, Blinken says
item.description: AQABA, Jordan (AP) — American officials have been in direct contact with the terrorist-designated rebel group that led the overthrow of Syrian President Bashar Assad, U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken said Saturday. Blinken, speaking at a news conference in Jordan, was the first U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation
